description: v1.3.0 perf baseline. Captured against the two-cluster federation fixture (kubeatlas-prod + kubeatlas-staging kind clusters, each loaded with the stress-test-5k generator). /federation/graph supports two zoom levels: 'resource' (the default flat union — every resource across the named members) and 'cluster' (one summary Node per cluster with a resource count and a top-N kind summary). The resource-level latency is JSON-marshal-bound at this scale; the cluster-level summary is the small-payload form intended for the federation landing page and runs ~200x faster on the same fixture. Single-cluster numbers are unchanged from v1.2.0 and continue to live in perf-baseline-v1.2.json.

captured_on.runtime: kubeatlas binary, KUBEATLAS_BACKEND=memory, multicluster.enabled=true, two kind clusters via Docker Desktop on WSL2
